passageNotes: 9.24.3 LIGHTS, BUOYS AND WAYPOINTS (Reeds Area 24 – Portugal, p.1000). Section covers navigational aids from Rio Miño (Spanish border) to Lisboa. Entries listed by locality: Rio Miño (Spanish Border) to Lisboa — Viana do Castelo, Póvoa de Varzim, Leixões, Rio Douro/Porto, Aveiro, Figueira da Foz, Nazaré, São Martinho do Porto, Los Farilhões/Ilha da Berlenga, Peniche, Lisboa to Cape St Vincent — Cascais. Montedor light: Fl(2) 9.5s 102m 22M, Twr, R cupola and bldg; 41°45'·07N 08°52'·41W. Cabo Mondego: Fl 5s 96m 28M; W twr and house; 40°11'·46N 08°54'·31W. Ponta de São Antônio: Iso R 6s 32m 9M, W col, R bands; 39°30'·61N 09°08'·61W (NE side of ent). Farilhão Grande: Fl(2) 5s 99m 13M; 39°28'·73N 09°32'·72W. Ilha da Berlenga: Fl 10s 120m 16M; W twr and houses; 39°24'·90N 09°30'·60W. Cabo Carvoeiro: Fl R 6s 56m 15M; W twr; 39°21'·63N 09°24'·47W. Cabo de Roca: Fl(4) 17s 164m 26M; W twr and bldgs; 38°46'·90N 09°29'·84W. Cabo Raso: Fl(2) 5s 22m 15M; 38°42'·57N 09°29'·15W. Cascais Ldg lts 284.7°: Front, Forte de Santa Marta Oc WR 6s 24m W18M, R14M.

9.24.3 LIGHTS, BUOYS AND WAYPOINTS continued (Reeds Area 24 – Portugal, p.1001). Localities covered: Rio Tejo N Bank (Marconi Iso WR 3s 17m 9M; Oeiras marina; Doca de Paço de Arcos; Cabo Espichel Fl 4s 167m 26M; 38°24'·92N 09°12'·97W), Lisboa (Triple ldg lts 047.1°; Esteiro Oc R 6s 81m 21M; Racon Q 15M 38°42'·23N 09°15'·59W; Rear Marconi Oc R 6s 153m 21M; Forte Bugio Fl G 5s 27m 12M twr on fortress 38°39'·63N 09°17'·93W; Lisnave Fl G 2s 4m 3M; Cabo Espichel Fl 4s 167m 26M), Sesimbra (Cavalo Fort Oc 5s 34m 14M), Setúbal (Ldg lts 039.7° both Iso Y 6s 12/60m 22M; Racon B 15M 38°27'·22N 08°58'·45W), Sines (Cabo de Sines Fl(2) 15s 55m 26M; W twr and house; 37°57'·57N 08°52'·82W). Cape St Vincent/Sagres section: Cabo de São Vicente Fl 5s 84m 32M; Aeromarine 37°01'·36N 08°59'·78W. Lagos: Pta da Piedade Fl 7s 50m 20M; 37°04'·81N 08°40'·14W. Portimão: Ponta do Altar Fl LFl 5s 31m 16M; W twr and bldg; 37°06'·34N 08°31'·17W. Albufeira: Ponta da Baleeira Oc 6s 30m 11M; 37°04'·81N 08°15'·80W. Vilamoura: Vilamoura Fl 5s 17m 19M; 37°04'·46N 08°07'·37W. Faro/Olhão and Tavira section. Vila Real de Santo Antonio: Lt ho Fl 6s 51m 26M; W twr, B bands; 37°11'·20N 07°24'·91W.

9.24.4 PASSAGE INFORMATION (Reeds Area 24 – Portugal, p.1002). Bibliography: SW Spain & Portugal CC (Fernhurst/Jens); Atlantic Spain and Portugal (Imray/RCC/Buchanan); W coasts of Spain and Portugal Pilot (NP 67). Wind and Weather: Portuguese Trades (Nortada) are N'ly F4-6 in summer; Portugal Current runs S at ½–¾kn. Summer gales rare; coastal fog common mornings. Make passages between 0400 and 1200 to avoid stronger afternoon winds. Tidal streams set N on flood and S on ebb but poorly documented. Port Closures: Some harbours closed in strong onshore winds or heavy swell — dangerous breaking seas at harbour entrance or bar. Signals: cylinder/ball hoisted close up = closed; half-mast = enter with caution. By night: two vertical steady or flashing = caution. Spanish Border to Cabo Raso: Rio Miño (Minho) 15M S of Cabo Silleiro forms northern border. Coastline hilly as far S as Porto, then low and sandy, backed by pine forests, to Cabo Carvoeiro. Viana do Castelo is a commercial and fishing port with marina at NE end; Windfloat Atlantic (floating wind farm, 3 turbines with buoyed restricted area) 12M due W of harbour entrance; 6.5M NNW of Póvoa de Varzim. Leixões is industrial port 2M N of mouth of R Douro; may be closed in heavy swell; seaplane operating area with no-entry zone N of Leixões; new marina 3M upriver at Oporto. Aveiro and Figueira da Foz exposed to W; can be closed in bad weather. Cabo Carvoeiro is W tip of Peniche peninsula; Ilha da Berlenga and Os Farilhões 5 and 9.5M NW of Cabo Carvoeiro; within 'Area to be avoided' (ITZ). Coastal route between Cabo Carvoeiro and Berlenga 5M wide, deep and clear. Peniche fishing port/marina on S side of peninsula well sheltered from N'lies but open to SW swell. Off Cabo da Roca Traffic Separation Scheme: 14M to seaward, extends further 20M W with extra lanes for dangerous cargoes; ITZ is 14M wide. Cabo Raso to Cape St Vincent: Cascais is convenient stop 12M E of Lisboa; Oeiras Marina 5M E of Cascais also good. Barra Norte (depth 5m) joins main Barra Sul abeam São Julião for Rio Tejo entry. In Lisboa 5 marinas line N bank of R Tejo. Sesimbra well sheltered from N'lies. Setúbal few yacht facilities. Sines is strategically placed commercial port 56M N of Cabo São Vicente; small marina limited facilities. Tiny bay (37°17'·51N 08°52'·08W) below high cliffs of Punta da Arrifana gives shelter from N'lies. Southern Portugal – The Algarve: ITZ extends 15M W/SW of Cape St Vincent; TSS 20M further west. Passage anchorages sheltered from N'lies at Enseada de Belixe (E of Cabo São Vicente) and Enseada das Sagres and da Baleeira (NE of Pta de Sagres). Direct route to Gibraltar 110°/175M (Cabo São Vicente to Tarifa). Good marinas at Lagos, Portimão, Albufeira and Vilamoura. Tuna farm nets problem NE of Ponta de Sagres (Baia de Lagos SW of Portimão) and 4M SSW the entrance to Faro/Olhão at Cabo de Santa Maria (AC 89, 93) — dimly lit and hard to see against shore lights; stay outside 50m contour. Lobster pot markers also a major hazard along Algarve coast. Marina at Vila Real de Santo António is on Portuguese bank of Rio Guadiana (Spanish border).
region: Portugal – Viana do Castelo to Spanish Border (Algarve)

passageNotes: 9.24.5 SPECIAL NOTES FOR PORTUGAL (Reeds Area 24 – Portugal, p.1003). Charts: Two folios FA (antigo, 2-digit nos) and F94 (5-digit nos); 4 offshore charts 23201-04; 6 coastal charts 24201-06; 12 harbour charts 26301-12; 8 harbour approach charts 26401-08; 3 charts of harbour plans 27501-03. Leisure (recreio) charts at 1:150,000 scale Nos 25R01-12 cover whole coast N–S. Portuguese chart agents: J Garraio & Ca Lda, ave 24 de Julho, 2-1st Dto, 1200-478 Lisboa; tel 213 473 081, info@jgarraio.pt, www.jgarraio.pt. Portuguese Hydrographic Office: www.ihpt.pt. Standard Time: UT (NOT UT–1); add 1hr for DST from last Sun in Mar to Sat before last Sun in Oct. Representation: Portuguese Tourist Office, 11 Belgrave Square, London SW1X 8PP; tel 020 7201 6666. Tourist Office, Ave António Augusto de Aguiar 86, P-1000 Lisboa; tel 213 425 231. British Embassy, Rua de S. Bernardo 33, 1249-082 Lisboa; tel 21 359 4082. Consulates in Lisbon/Portimão and Hon Consuls at Oporto and Ponta Delgada (Azores); tel 808 203537. Telephone: To call Portugal from UK dial +351, area code, then number. To call UK from Portugal dial +44 area code less initial 0, then number. Area code is mandatory. National emergency 112; National SAR 214 401 919. R/T: VHF Ch 09 is recreational craft channel used by all Portuguese marinas, in addition to Port VHF channels. Access: Daily flights in season to/from UK via Porto, Lisboa and Faro with connections by bus/train to other towns. See Area 25 for Spanish flights. Currency: Euro; VAT (IVA) 23%; credit cards widely accepted; cash dispensers in all towns. Public Holidays: Jan 1; Shrove Tues; Good Friday; Apr 25 (Liberation Day); May 1 (Labour Day); Jun 10 (Camões Day); Aug 15 (Assumption); Dec 8 (Immaculate Conception); Dec 25 (Christmas). Also every town has a local festa. Documents: Portugal observes EU regulations; formalities may be lengthy. Passports; crew list on headed paper with yacht's rubber stamp; DoB and passport nos where joined/departed; Certificate of Competence (Yachtmaster Offshore, ICC/HOCC etc); Radio Operator's certificate; EHIC. Yacht: Registration certificate; proof of VAT status (original VAT invoice or copy, and T2L C88 Status form); Marine insurance; Ship's Radio licence; Itinerary backed up by ship's log.

name: Viana do Castelo
approachNote: WPT 41°40'·18N 08°49'·37W; Oc WRG 4s to abeam No 3 SHM Lt buoy; track 012·5° on ldg line (which applies solely to shipyard/FV area). Outer mole head (Fl R 3s) should be given wide clearance; best water lies E of No 2 buoy. Flood reaches 2kn max; ebb 3kn but 6kn if river in spate. Entry to harbour prohibited during seaplane firefighting operations, 3.3M SSW of Outer Mole. Windfloat Atlantic floating wind farm (3 turbines with buoyed restricted area) lies 12M due W of Viana do Castelo.
shelter: Excellent in marina; possible strong current across its entrance. No anchoring in river. Night entry not advised if any swell.

marina.name: Marina de Viana do Castelo
marina.notes: 200 inc berths; €2.60 up to 12m LOA; berths abeam Nos 13 and 14 buoys, 200m short of low (4.6m) and noisy road/rail bridge. Small pedestrian bridge across marina entrance lifts on request; if shut berth on waiting pontoon, bridge remains open 0000–0800. Limited berths F&A on W side of first pontoon D. VHF Ch 09; HM Ch 09; MRCC (21) 4401919; Police 822345; tel 823346; Brit Consul 808 203 537; Port HM 829096; call marina Ch 16 (0900-1200; 1400-1700LT). YC on Marina Ch 09. Charts: AC 3633/4, 3257; PC 24201, 26401; SC 41B. Standard Port LISBOA; ML 2.0. No sign of marina planned in the low field basin (41°41'·34N 08°50'·0W).
